1: The horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
2: My father's God, and I will exalt him.
3: The LORD LORD is is his name.
4: His chosen captains also are drowned in the Red sea.
5: They sank into the bottom as a stone.
6: Thy right hand, O LORD LORD , hath dashed in pieces the enemy.
7: Thou sentest forth thy wrath, which which consumed them as stubble.
8: And And the depths were congealed in the heart of the sea.
9: I will draw my sword, my hand shall destroy them.
10: They sank as lead in the mighty waters.
11: Fearful in in praises, doing wonders?
13: Thou hast guided them them in thy strength unto thy holy habitation.
14: Sorrow shall take hold on the inhabitants of Palestina.
15: All the inhabitants of Canaan shall melt away.
16: Till the people pass over, which which thou hast purchased.
17: In In the sanctuary, O LORD LORD , which which thy hands have established.
18: The LORD LORD shall reign for ever and ever.
19: For the horse of Pharaoh went in with his chariots and with his horsemen into the sea, and the LORD LORD brought again the waters of the sea upon them; but the children of Israel went on dry land land in the midst of the sea.
20: And Miriam the prophetess, the sister of Aaron, took a timbrel in her hand; and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.
21: The horse and his rider hath he thrown into the sea.
22: So Moses brought Israel from the Red sea, and they went out into the wilderness of Shur; and they went three days in the wilderness, and found no water.
23: And when they came to Marah, they could not drink of the waters of Marah, for they were were bitter: therefore the name of it was called Marah.
24: And the people murmured against Moses, saying, What shall we drink?
25: And he cried unto the LORD LORD ; and the LORD LORD shewed him a tree, which which when he had cast into the waters, the waters were made sweet: there he made for them a statute and an ordinance, and there he proved them,
26: and said, If thou wilt diligently hearken to the voice of the LORD LORD thy God, and wilt do that which is right in his sight, and wilt give ear to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of these diseases upon thee, which I have brought upon the Egyptians: for I am am the LORD LORD that healeth thee.
27: And they came to Elim, where were were twelve wells of water, and threescore and ten palm trees: and they encamped there by the waters.
